news.headline: Moneta Ventures raises over $250M fund for U.S. West Coast and Texas startups; focuses on Seed and Series A enterprise software companies.
news.summary: In Austin, Texas, Moneta Ventures, with headquarters in both Sacramento, CA, and Austin, TX, has successfully secured over $250 million for its third fund. This venture capital firm specializes in supporting Seed and Series A enterprise software companies and follows a unique investment strategy known as "Software++". This approach focuses on identifying software companies with advanced monetization structures and innovative technology solutions. Noteworthy investors in their latest fund round include...

news.headline: Apple production hubs affected by new tariffs, causing revenue drop by 20%, according to reports.
Healthpeak Properties (NYSE: DOC) to release Q1 2025 financial results on April 24 after NYSE trading close. Conference call on April 25 at 8:00 am MT (10:00 am ET).

news.headline: Trivest partners with Sentinel Dock & Door Solutions, merging 3 regional providers in Canada for growth, who appoint Travis Allan as CEO.
news.summary: Miami-based private equity firm Trivest Partners has recently established Sentinel Dock & Door Solutions, a prominent network of commercial dock and door service providers in Canada. This new venture combines three top regional service providers into one unified platform, positioning them for further expansion. Alongside this launch, Trivest has appointed Travis Allan as the new Chief Executive Officer of Sentinel.

Those interested in participating must register for the call via the provided link to obtain the dial-in number and personal pin for access.

Barco and CJ 4DPLEX form 5-year deal for 1,000 Barco laser projectors to expand SCREENX worldwide. #CinemaCon2025
news.summary: Today at CinemaCon 2025 in Las Vegas, it was revealed that Barco, a top provider of laser-powered cinema technology, and CJ 4DPLEX, a global leader in premium film formats, have signed a worldwide deal. This agreement involves the installation of as many as 1,000 Barco laser projectors.

news.headline: Natural Gas Services Group appoints Anthony Gallegos to Board of Directors, filling vacancy by David Bradshaw retires in Dec.
news.summary: In Midland, Texas, on April 3, 2025, Natural Gas Services Group, Inc. (NYSE: NGS), a prominent supplier of natural gas compression equipment and services in the energy sector, revealed the appointment of Anthony Gallegos to its Board of Directors. Gallegos assumes the role previously held by David Bradshaw following his retirement from the Board in December 2024. This strategic decision was made on April 1, 2025, marking a significant change within the company's leadership.

news.headline: Equifax to release Q1 2025 results on April 22 at 6:30 a.m. ET, followed by conference call at 8:30 a.m. ET.
news.summary: Equifax (NYSE: EFX) is set to reveal its first-quarter financial results for the period ending March 31, 2025, through a press release on April 22 at 6:30 a.m. ET. Following this, a conference call will be held at 8:30 a.m. ET on the same day.

news.headline: Guess?, Inc. (NYSE: GES) reports 5% revenue growth in USD and 9% in constant currency in Q4 2025. CEO cites rag & bone acquisition as key driver.
news.summary: In Los Angeles, Guess?, Inc. (NYSE: GES) released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full fiscal year ending on February 1, 2025. CEO Carlos Alberini noted a 5% revenue growth in U.S. dollars and a 9% growth in constant currency during the fourth quarter. The increase was mainly attributed to the rag & bone acquisition, alongside strong performance in wholesale operations in Europe and the Americas, and expanded licensing opportunities.

news.headline: Guess? Inc. has appointed Alberto Toni as CFO, replacing Dennis Secor. Toni is currently CFO of Flos B&B Italia Group.
news.summary: In Los Angeles, Guess?, Inc. (NYSE: GES) revealed that Alberto Toni, the current Group Managing Director and CFO of Flos B&B Italia Group S.p.A., will take on the role of CFO of Guess? following the completion of the Company’s next Quarterly Report, due around mid-June. Alberto Toni will replace Dennis Secor, the Interim CFO, who will continue as Executive Vice President at the Company until September.

news.summary: Ag Growth International Inc. will be conducting a conference call on Tuesday, May 6, 2025, at 8:00 am ET to review its performance for the three-month period that concluded on March 31, 2025. The company, listed on the TSX as AFN, will release the results after the markets close on Monday, May 5, 2025. Interested individuals can access the event through the AGI First Quarter Results webcast link or by dialing +1-833-821-0159.

news.headline: Energy Transfer to release Q1 2025 earnings on May 6, 2025, after market closes. Earnings call set for 3:30 pm CST/ 4:30 pm EST.

news.headline: Cryptocurrency prices drop on tariff news; Bitcoin $82,025.41 (-5.1%), Ethereum $1,785.61 (-6.5%), Solana $115.31 (-11.7%). Trading lower due to decline in financial markets.
news.summary: Cryptocurrency markets are experiencing a downturn today as traditional financial markets also see declines. Some of the top cryptocurrencies are facing losses, with Bitcoin dropping by 5.1% to $82,025.41, Ethereum down 6.5% at $1,785.61, Solana decreasing by 11.7% to $115.31, XRP falling 4.6% to $2.05, Dogecoin losing 8.5% at $0.1587, and Shiba Inu down 3.6% to $0.00001221.Important statistics reveal a 7.5% increase in large transaction volume and a 6% decline in daily active addresses. The number of transactions over $100,000 has decreased from 10,387 to 10,152 in a day, while exchange netflows have risen by 19.4%. Additionally, 194,417 traders were liquidated in the past 24 hours, resulting in losses totaling $527.37 million.In recent developments, experts are considering Bitcoin as a feasible global reserve asset, while JPMorgan notes gold surpassing Bitcoin in inflation hedge strategy. Overall, since the beginning of 2025, Bitcoin, Ethereum, and XRP have seen declines. For further details, the complete story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Fortive to host Q1 2025 earnings conference call on May 1, 2025 at 12:00 p.m. ET, lasting about 1 hour. Website: www.fortive.com.
news.summary: Fortive Corporation (NYSE: FTV) revealed it will broadcast their first quarter 2025 earnings conference call on May 1, 2025, at 12:00 p.m. ET, lasting around 1 hour. The conference call and slide presentation will be accessible on the "Investors" segment of Fortive’s website, www.fortive.com, under "Events/Presentations." Following the call, a replay will be posted on the same website section.

news.headline: Impinj set to reveal Q1 2025 financial results.
news.summary: In the latest report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 3.9%, marking a 0.2% decrease from last month. The total number of jobs added in various sectors reached 250,000, surpassing market expectations. Additionally, the GDP growth for the last quarter was reported at 5.2%, showing a significant improvement from the previous year. Experts attribute this positive economic trend to increased consumer spending and investments in infrastructure projects. The stock market also saw a boost, with the main index rising by 2.5% in response to these developments. Overall, the data indicates a strengthening economy and positive outlook for the upcoming months.

news.headline: Astronics provides advanced frequency converter for NASA-Boeing X-66 Demonstrator Aircraft.
news.summary: In East Aurora, New York, Astronics Corporation (Nasdaq: ATRO) has been selected to supply an advanced frequency converter for the NASA-Boeing X-66 Demonstrator Aircraft.

news.headline: Procurant introduces tariff charge management feature for grocery buyers, aiding in tracking and managing import tariffs within their purchase orders and invoices. Now buyers can set up and manage tariff charge rules.
news.summary: Procurant, a software solutions provider for the food and retail sector, unveiled a new feature in its Procurant Commerce platform in Watsonville, Calif. The update allows retail grocery buyers to efficiently handle tariff-related fees. Through this enhancement, users can now easily monitor, control, and generate reports on import tariffs associated with their orders and invoices. Specifically, Procurant buyers can establish and adjust tariff charge regulations and implement tariff rates during their purchasing process. This development equips retail grocery buyers with advanced capabilities to manage tariff-related expenses effectively.

news.headline: Greenbrier raises quarterly dividend to $0.32/share, 7% increase from $0.30. Payable to stockholders on May 13, 2025.
news.summary: Lake Oswego, Oregon- On April 3, 2025, The Greenbrier Companies (NYSE: GBX) disclosed news of a quarterly cash dividend rising to $0.32 per share, to be distributed on May 13, 2025. This increase marks a 7% rise from the previous $0.30 per share dividend. Stockholders as of April 22, 2025, will be eligible for this dividend.

news.headline: CACI awarded $54M task order to support U.S. Army with advanced ground sensors, under DoDIAC contract.
news.summary: CACI International Inc (NYSE: CACI) from Reston, Va. revealed a new five-year task order worth up to $54 million. This task order aims to sustain the U.S. Army Product Manager Ground Sensors (PM GS) under the Department of Defense Information Analysis Center's (DoDIAC) multiple-award contract (MAC) vehicle. CACI's role involves enhancing operational capability and efficiency for crucial ground sensors, encompassing night vision equipment, throughout their life cycle.

news.headline: BWXT to release first-quarter results on May 5, 2025, with a conference call at 5pm EDT. Dial-in numbers: (U.S.) 1-888-596-4144, (International) 1-646-968-2525.
news.summary: BWX Technologies, Inc. (NYSE: BWXT) is set to announce its first quarter 2025 results on May 5, 2025, after the market closes. A conference call will be held at 5:00 p.m. EDT on the same day. Interested individuals can join as listen-only participants and access the presentation online at investors.bwxt.com. For those wishing to dial in, the numbers are (U.S.) 1-888-596-4144 and (International) 1-646-968-2525, using conference ID: 8426324. Following the call, a replay will be available.

news.headline: Canadian General Investments (CGI) reports $63.60 NAV per share on Mar 31, 2025, with -7.9% YTD and 2.8% 12-month returns compared to benchmark S&P/TSX Composite Index's 1.5% and 15.8% returns.
news.summary: In Toronto, Canada, Canadian General Investments, Limited (CGI) announced that as of March 31, 2025, their net asset value per share (NAV) stood at $63.60 on an unaudited basis. Year-to-date, the NAV returns with dividends reinvested were -7.9%, while the 12-month NAV returns were 2.8%. In comparison, the benchmark S&P/TSX Composite Index had returns of 1.5% and 15.8% for the same periods, respectively. The CGI is listed on both the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX: CGI) and the London Stock Exchange (LSE: CGI).

news.headline: Trump Trade Adviser Navarro: Tariff rates are half of the sum of all cheating.
National Fuel's Utility President Donna DeCarolis retiring, will stay as advisor; new exec not mentioned.
news.summary: Donna DeCarolis, the President of a utility company, is set to retire from her current position. However, she will still remain connected to the energy sector by serving as a Senior Energy Advisor to National Fuel. This transition reflects a shift in leadership within the company, as they prepare for new guidance and direction in their operations. DeCarolis' extensive expertise and experience will be a valuable asset in her new advisory role, ensuring continued strategic insights and support for the energy company. This news marks a significant change within the utility sector, with DeCarolis' continued involvement highlighting the importance of her knowledge and contributions.

news.headline: Trump Trade Adviser Navarro: Tariff calculations are based on sophisticated analysis.
Concern over cancellation of NEH grants by April 2; American Council of Learned Societies and more express dismay.
news.summary: On April 3, 2025, three prominent academic organizations, including the American Council of Learned Societies, the Council of Graduate Schools, and The Phi Beta Kappa Society, expressed serious apprehension regarding recent actions by the National Endowment for the Humanities (NEH). Specifically, notices issued on April 2 indicated the cancellation of grants by the NEH, raising concerns among scholars and educators alike. The exact number of grants affected and the potential implications of this decision remain undisclosed, prompting these organizations to voice their distress at the situation.

news.headline: TILT Holdings acknowledged default notices from IIP subsidiaries IIP-PA 9 LLC and IIP-MA 8 LLC on March 28, 2025.
news.summary: On April 3, 2025, TILT Holdings Inc. disclosed receiving default notices from Innovative Industrial Properties, including from subsidiaries IIP-PA 9 LLC and IIP-MA 8 LLC. TILT is a leading global provider of cannabis solutions covering various aspects such as inhalation technologies, cultivation, manufacturing, processing, brand development, and retail. The notices were received after the market closed on March 28, 2025. The company's stock is listed as Cboe CA:TILT and OTCQB: TLLTF.

news.headline: Fitell Corporation got a Nasdaq notification for its shares closing below $1 for 30 days, risking delisting.
news.summary: Fitell Corporation (Nasdaq: FTEL), an Australian online retailer of gym and fitness equipment, received a Notification Letter from Nasdaq stating that for the past 30 business days, the closing bid price of the Company’s ordinary shares was below $1.00 per share. This falls short of the minimum closing bid price required for continued listing on The Nasdaq Capital Market under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(a)(2).

news.headline: FutureFuel Corp. appoints Pamela R. Butcher as a new Director, increasing their Board to nine members.
news.summary: FutureFuel Corp., a manufacturer of chemicals and biofuels, has expanded its Board of Directors to nine members. Effective April 3, 2025, Pamela R. Butcher was appointed as a Class C member of the Board. Ms. Butcher will serve until a successor is elected or in case of death, resignation, or removal. This move comes as the company, trading under NYSE: FF, aims to strengthen its governance structure and leadership team.

Volkswagen to impose import fee on US-sold cars due to 25% tariffs by Trump, impacting auto prices.
news.summary: One of the initial and most evident instances of car manufacturers responding to the 25 percent tariffs enforced by President Trump on imported automobiles and auto components is the company’s decision to implement price hikes as a countermeasure.

news.headline: DigitalOcean to host Investor Day in New York on April 4, 2025 at 9 a.m. ET. CEO Paddy Srinivasan to present. Details at investors.digitalocean.com.
